1:887.name: Configuration=Medium, Elevation=Flat
1:887.description:
Collapsing top app bars can be used for longer titles, to house imagery, or to provide a stronger presence to the top app bar.


[GDP / GM3 Design System] 🔍

1:174.name: Type=Round, Size=Small, Width=Default, State=Enabled
1:174.description:
Standard Icon buttons are low-emphasis buttons.
They are used for the lowest priority actions, especially when presenting multiple options.

Icon buttons can be placed on a variety of backgrounds. Until the button is interacted with, its container isn’t visible.

1:1202.name: Type=Round, Size=Small, Width=Default, State=Enabled
1:1202.description:
Standard Icon buttons are low-emphasis buttons.
They are used for the lowest priority actions, especially when presenting multiple options.

Icon buttons can be placed on a variety of backgrounds. Until the button is interacted with, its container isn’t visible.

1:1066.name: play_arrow_filled
1:1066.description:
Play, Start, Begin
Use for: Starting or resuming playback

1:1743.name: State=Enabled
1:1743.description: Cards are versatile containers, holding anything from images to headlines, supporting text, buttons, lists, and other components.

1:882.name: App bar
1:882.description:
Use a top app bar to provide content and actions related to a current screen, such as navigation, screen headlines, and actions.


Focus on describing the current page and provide 1–2 essential actions.
Displays labels and page navigation controls at the top of the page. (Use a toolbar to display page actions)
Three types: small, medium flexible, large flexible
On scroll, apply a fill color to separate from body content
Can animate on and off screen with another bar of controls, like a row of chips


Guidelines

1:1012.name: Carousel
1:1012.description:
A Carousel contains a collection of items that can be scrolled on and off the screen.

🛠️
Select between Uniform and Non-uniform layouts;
Choose Mobile or Tablet context
An optional item-text’s visibility can be toggled on and off
